senses.definition: in a way related to bolography, a term not widely used in standard English and likely specialized or erroneous
examples: The diagram was described bolographically, though the term is uncommon.
She annotated the manuscript bolographically, a practice seen in some niche circles.

senses.definition: a plural form of bolograph, a largely unknown or invented term; may refer to items in a niche or speculative field
examples: The collection includes several bolographs from the early experiments.
Scholars debated the authenticity of the bolographs.

senses.definition: a term that appears to be rare or speculative; may refer to a fictional or invented science of bolographs
examples: Bolography was described in the manuscript, but its meaning is unclear.
Some readers treat bolography as a metaphor for complex documentation.

senses.definition: a nonstandard or misspelled form related to bolo; could be a typographical variant or fictional term
examples: The text contains boloing errors that obscure the meaning.
Boloing appears to be an error rather than a recognized term.

senses.definition: a rare or invented noun possibly referring to a belief, practice, or symbol called boloism
examples: Some scholars discussed boloism as a cultural motif in the text.
The concept of boloism remained obscure to most readers.

senses.definition: a person associated with or devoted to bolos (plural: bolomen); likely a fictional or niche term
examples: The boloman led the expedition with a steady hand.
In the story, bolomen defend the village at dawn.

senses.definition: plural of boloman; a fictional or niche term referring to people associated with bolos
examples: The bolomen formed a circle around the camp.
Two bolomen stood sentry while others prepared tools.

senses.definition: an instrument that measures very small changes in temperature by detecting electrical resistance changes
examples: A bolometer detects tiny temperature variations in the detector.
The lab uses a bolometer to study infrared radiation.

senses.definition: plural of bolometer; multiple instruments that measure tiny temperature changes
examples: The package includes two bolometers for redundancy.
Bolometers are key components in some infrared cameras.

senses.definition: related to or using a bolometer or the measurement of heat by radiation
examples: Bolometric detectors measure heat changes due to radiation.
The study used bolometric methods for high precision temperature reading.

senses.definition: in a bolometric (heat-detecting) manner; using bolometer-based methods
examples: The sensor responded bolometrically to the infrared pulse.
Measurements were bolometrically analyzed.

senses.definition: plural of bolometry; the study or measurement of radiation-induced heat
examples: The bolometries of the instrument exceeded expectations.
Researchers compared bolometries across several detectors.

senses.definition: the science or technique of measuring heat changes caused by radiation, commonly using bolometers
examples: Bolometry is essential in infrared astronomy.
The team studied bolometry to improve sensor sensitivity.

senses.definition: a misspelling or informal variant of baloney; a type of sausage or an informal term for nonsense
examples: That story is a bunch of boloney.
They served sliced boloney in the cafeteria.

senses.definition: plural of boloney; slices of boloney sausage or the informal term used metaphorically
examples: The lunch included two boloneys on white bread.
His excuses were boloneys, not worth listening to.

senses.definition: a term that appears obscure; possibly a misspelling or fictional root word
examples: The manuscript mentions boloroot in a coded section.
Experts debated the meaning of boloroot.

senses.definition: plural of bolo; could refer to a type of knot or weapon, or be a fictional term
examples: They tied the strings with several bolos.
In the story, the bolos are ceremonial cords.

senses.definition: a member of the Bolshevik party, a political group that led the Russian Revolution in 1917
examples: The bolshevik became a leading figure after the revolution.
Bolsheviks advocated for a socialist state.

senses.definition: plural of bolshevik; multiple members of the Bolshevik faction
examples: The bolsheviki debated strategy at the meeting.
Many bolsheviki were veterans of the revolutionary movement.

senses.definition: relating to Bolshevism or Bolshevik characteristics
examples: The manifesto had bolshevikian rhetoric.
Some scholars note bolshevikian influences in the text.

senses.definition: plural of bolshevik; multiple members of the Bolshevik party
examples: The bolsheviks seized power in 1917.
Different bolsheviks had varying visions for the future.

senses.definition: the political ideology of the Bolsheviks, advocating for a socialist state led by the working class
examples: Bolshevism influenced many later communist movements.
Critics argued that bolshevism led to authoritarian rule.

senses.definition: plural of bolshevism; variations or manifestations of bolshevik ideology
examples: Several bolshevisms emerged in different regions.
Debates covered the different bolshevisms within the movement.

senses.definition: a Bolshevik; a member of the Bolshevik party
examples: The bolshevist addressed the crowd.
Many bolshevists faced tough decisions during the upheaval.

senses.definition: relating to Bolshevism or Bolshevik style of thinking
examples: The speech had bolshevistic overtones.
Critics dismissed the bolshevistic rhetoric as propagandistic.
